title: Journey
description: When you **travel by a safe route**, through safe or dangerous lands, indicate your destination on the map. The GM will tell you how long the trip takes, and what—if anything—happens along the way. When you reach your destination, choose someone to **Manage Provisions** to determine how many rations were consumed over the course of the trip.

title: Undertake a Perilous Journey
description:
When you **travel through dangerous lands, and not on a safe route**, indicate the course you want to take on the map and ask the GM how far you should be able to get before needing to Make Camp. If you're exploring with no set destination, indicate which way you go.

Then, choose one party member to **Scout Ahead**, and one to **Navigate**, resolving those moves in that order.

title: Forage (+WIS)
description: When you **spend a day seeking food in the wild**, and your surroundings are not barren, roll +WIS.

outcomes.text: You gain 1d4 rations, +1d4 rations if you have the knowledge and gear needed to trap or hunt.
The 10+ result applies, but you must also first face a **Discovery** or **Danger** of the GM's choice.
Mark XP, and GM makes a move.

title: Make Camp
description:
When you **settle in to rest**, choose one member of the party to **Manage Provisions**. Then, if you eat and drink and have enough XP, you may level up.

When you bed down in dangerous lands, decide on a watch order. Then, the GM chooses one person on watch during the night to roll +nothing:

outcomes.text: The night passes without incident.
The GM chooses 1:
bullets: The person on watch notices a nearby **Discovery**
One party member of the GM's choice suffers a restless night
One or more followers causes trouble
A **Danger** approaches—it's not immediately hostile, but whoever's on watch had better **Stay Sharp** anyway

outcomes.text: Everyone marks XP, and a **Danger** manifests. You'd better **Stay Sharp**!

submoves.title: When You Wake
submoves.description: When you wake from at least a few hours of uninterrupted sleep, and you ate and drank the night before, heal damage equal to half of your max HP.

title: Stay Sharp (+WIS)
description: When you **are on watch and something approaches**, roll +WIS.

outcomes.text: You notice in time to alert everyone and prepare a response—all party members take **+1 forward**.
You manage to sound the alarm, but no one has time to prepare.
Mark XP, and whatever approaches has the drop on you.

title: Hunker Down
description: When you **take shelter to wait out the elements,** choose 1 party member to roll +nothing.

outcomes.text: It doesn't take long for things to clear up.
Things aren't going to change any time soon. You can **Forge Ahead** or **Make Camp** here for the night and hope things have changed by morning.
Mark XP, and GM makes a move.

title: Forge Ahead (+Constitution)
description: When you **push on despite powerful opposition from the elements**, roll +CON.  If you make progress, ask the GM where you end up on the map, and if the weather shows any sign of relenting.

outcomes.text: You go as far as you are able before needing to pause for a rest.
Choose 1:
bullets: You go as far as you are able, but overtax yourself and become *weak*, *shaky* or *sick* (choose one)
You go as far as you are able, but the weather takes its deepest toll on your gear (ask the GM how)
On second thought, maybe your're better off staying put.

outcomes.text: Mark XP, and GM makes a move.

title: Scout Ahead (+WIS)
description: When you **take point and look for anything out of the ordinary**, roll +WIS.

outcomes.text: Choose 2:
bullets: You get the drop on whatever lies ahead
You discern a beneficial aspect of the terrain—shortcut, shelter, or tactical advantage (describe it)
You make a **Discovery** (ask the GM)
You notice sign of a nearby **Danger**—ask the GM what it is, and what it might signify

outcomes.text: Choose 1 from the list above.
Mark XP, and GM makes a move.

bullets: The weather worsens
Scout attracts unwanted attention
Scout becomes lost
Scout encounters a **Danger**
Scout is ambushed
The rest of the party is ambushed
Scout is captured or trapped
Scout suffers an injury

title: Navigate (+INT)
description: When you **plot the best course through dangerous or unfamiliar lands**, roll +INT.

outcomes.text: You avoid dangers and distractions and make good time, reaching a point of the GM's choosing before you need to **Make Camp**.
The GM chooses 1:
bullets: You happen upon a **Discovery** missed by the scout
The going is slow, or you wander off course. The GM says which, and where you end up on the map
You encounter a **Danger**; whether or not you're surprised depends on whether the scout has the drop on it

outcomes.text: Mark XP, and GM makes a move.

bullets: The weather worsens
They're being followed
They must backtrack, losing valuable time
Someone slips and is injured
They stumble into a **Danger**
They get lost

title: Recruit
description:
When you **go looking to hire help**, tell the GM what you're offering and whom you're looking for, phrased in one of the following ways:
- A group of X (porters, guards, minstrels, angry farmers, etc.)
- A skilled X (guide, sage, burglar, bodyguard, etc.)

A group is a follower like any other, but with the *group* tag.

If the GM says you can't find that kind of help in a place like this, start over or move on. Otherwise, **roll +nothing and take +1 if you have a good reputation** in these parts.

outcomes.text: They're yours for the hiring.
The GM chooses 1:
bullets: They demand greater compensation, in coin or some other form
No one here fits the bill, but you hear of someone elsewhere who does
They have a need that must be met first (permission from someone else, a favor, etc.)
You can tell at a glance that they're less than ideal (ask the GM how)

outcomes.text: No one shows, but mark XP.

submoves.title: GM Note
submoves.description: The GM will choose or roll their specifics (Quality, Loyalty, Instinct, Cost, tags, etc.) as needed, to be discovered through play — which might involve you grilling them, right now.

title: Order Follower (+Loyalty)
description: When you **order or expect a follower to do something dangerous, degrading, or contrary to their Instinct**, roll +Loyalty.

outcomes.text: They do it, now.
They do it, but the GM picks one:
bullets: Decrease the follower's Loyalty by 1
They complain loudly, now or later, and demand something in return
Caution, laziness, or fear makes them take a long time to get it done

outcomes.text: Mark XP, and the GM makes a move.

title: Do Their Thing (+Quality)
description: When a **follower does something chancy within the scope of their tags or moves**, roll +Quality.

outcomes.text: They do it, as well as one could reasonably hope.
They do it, but there's an unforeseen cost, consequence, or limitation (ask the GM what).
Mark XP, and the GM makes a move.

submoves.title: Note:
submoves.description: When a follower does something chancy that falls beyond the scope of their tags or moves, or does anything on their own, the GM says what happens.

title: Call for Assistance
description: When a **follower helps you make a move that calls for a roll**, take +1 to that roll, but know that they will be exposed to any potential consequences.

submoves.title: Hack and Slash or Volley
submoves.description: When a follower helps you **Hack and Slash** or **Volley**, roll their damage die alongside your own, use the higher result, and add any damage bonuses you get on top of that.

submoves.title: Defend
submoves.description: When a follower helps you **Defend**, you can spend 1 hold to redirect an attack to them instead of yourself.

title: Pay Up
description: When you **pay a follower's Cost**, increase their Loyalty by 1 (to a maximum of +3). You can't trigger this move again until both you and your follower have **Made Camp** together.

title: Watch Them Go
description: When a **follower reaches -3 Loyalty**, they betray or abandon you at the next opportunity.

title: Manage Provisions (+WIS)
description: When you **prepare and distribute food for the party**, roll +WIS.

outcomes.text: Choose 1:
bullets: Careful management reduces the amount of rations consumed (ask the GM by how much)
The party consumes the expected amount and the food you prepare is excellent—describe it, and everyone who licks their lips takes **+1 forward**

outcomes.text: The party consumes the expected amount of rations (1 per person if Making Camp, 1 per person per day if making a Journey).
Mark XP, and GM makes a move.

bullets: They bicker over ration shares
Local animal life is attracted by food
Someone becomes weak until they eat 1 additional ration
Water is discovered to be tainted or poisonous
1d4 rations are lost due to spoilage or over-consumption
Someone gets food poisoning and becomes shaky and sick
A nearby **Danger** is attracted by food
